title: Contemporary American Poultry

jokes.text: Pierce, stop trying to coin the phrase, "streets ahead." Trying? Coined and minted. Been there, coined that. "streets ahead" is verbal wildfire.
jokes.setup: Character trying to make a phrase catch on gets called out
jokes.punchline: Pierce's over-confident defense with increasingly ridiculous claims
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce - delusional confidence about something trivial. Multiple escalating claims build the absurdity perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: No, I'm telling you, 'streets ahead' is going to be huge. I can feel it.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, that's not a thing.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Not a thing? It's the thing. I'm streets ahead of the curve on this one.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You just made that up five minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That's how language works. Someone invents it, and then it catches on. I invented 'the pizzazz.' I invented 'razzle dazzle.' This is no different.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You did not invent any of those things.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Wake up, sheeple! I've been inventing phrases since before you were born. I probably invented your name!

jokes.text: He specifically asked about me? - Mm-hmm. - Sure. When Annie brought you up, he specifically asked, 'who is that?' Abed. - Oh. - What? That's what he said.
jokes.setup: Shirley excitedly thinks the guy asked specifically about her
jokes.punchline: Abed's literal interpretation reveals the guy simply didn't know who she was
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed character moment - his literal-mindedness deflates Shirley's romantic hopes in the most matter-of-fact way possible

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: He asked for me specifically!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: He said, 'Where's the black woman?' Shirley, there are two black women in this group.

jokes.text: 'streets ahead' is verbal wildfire.
jokes.setup: Pierce continues promoting his phrase
jokes.punchline: Absurdly grandiose description of a nonsense phrase
jokes.explanation: Pierce's delusional marketing speak for his failed slang

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, 'fleeb juice' is going to be the next big thing. It's not just a phrase, it's a lifestyle. It's a revolution wrapped in a enigma, soaked in the tears of innovation, and garnished with the zest of pure entrepreneurial genius. When future generations look back at this moment, they'll realize 'fleeb juice' was the clarion call that awakened humanity from its slumber of mediocrity.

jokes.text: If you have to ask, you're street's fine.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what 'streets ahead' means
jokes.punchline: Pierce creates awkward opposite phrase on the spot
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Pierce doubling down badly. Economical and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: If you have to ask, you're streets behind.

jokes.text: If God were edible-- not that I'm catholic, But if it was cool to eat God, He'd be a chicken finger.
jokes.setup: Describing how good the chicken fingers are
jokes.punchline: Elaborate theological comparison with awkward religious qualifier
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny religious comparison that escalates perfectly, though could be more economical.

dialogue.speaker: Abed Nadir
dialogue.line: These chicken fingers are so good, they're like... they're like the Holy Communion of fried food.

dialogue.speaker: Abed Nadir
dialogue.line: I mean, not that I would know what that tastes like. I'm not religious. But if I were, I imagine it would taste like this.

jokes.text: And if you guys knew how they treated The animals that you are eating, You would eat them even faster Just to put them out of their misery And then you would throw up.
jokes.setup: Britta starts typical vegetarian lecture
jokes.punchline: Logic twisted to absurd conclusion that eating more meat is compassionate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta moment - starts preachy, accidentally advocates opposite position through flawed logic.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: You know, being a vegetarian isn't just about health. It's about compassion for animals. Every time you eat meat, you're contributing to their suffering.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta, that's a good point.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Right? So if we really care about animals, we should minimize their suffering by eating as many of them as possible before they have to live in cages.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not... that's the opposite of what you just said.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No, think about it. The more we eat them, the fewer there are to suffer. It's simple math.

jokes.text: He has diabetes. He can't even make it to the litter box-- Chicken run! Whoo! I'm afraid that I'm gonna have to put her to sleep.
jokes.setup: Britta sharing sad story about sick cat
jokes.punchline: Students completely ignore her emotional moment to chase chicken
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed cringe comedy - Britta's vulnerable moment steamrolled by chicken obsession.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: My cat was really sick, and I had to make the hardest decision of my life...

dialogue.speaker: Students
dialogue.line: Is that a chicken?!

dialogue.speaker: Students
dialogue.line: A chicken! Let's go!

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Wait, no, I'm trying to tell you something—

jokes.text: Oh, I can hear those fingers calling my name. "Troy! Troy!" Troy, did you get my text message? "did you get my text message?"
jokes.setup: Troy hearing chicken fingers call his name
jokes.punchline: Reveals he thinks text messages also call his name
jokes.explanation: Great Troy moment - childlike magical thinking applied consistently to different phenomena.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Did you hear that? The chicken fingers are calling my name.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Troy, that's just the fryer.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: No, I'm telling you, they're calling me. And text messages do it too.

jokes.text: I got to stop hanging out with her. She sounds like a chicken finger.
jokes.setup: Troy's earlier moment about hearing chicken fingers call his name
jokes.punchline: Now applies this logic to decide against friendship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows Troy's consistent absurd logic - economical and perfectly character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: If the chicken fingers are calling my name, that's a sign. A sign that I should not be friends with anyone.

jokes.text: Well... Throat surgery might humanize you, But it doesn't make this acceptable.
jokes.setup: Someone presumably had throat surgery and can't speak
jokes.punchline: Backhanded acknowledgment that surgery makes them more likable but still criticizes
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about someone's medical condition while still being critical - uncomfortable but funny.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: Look, I'm glad your surgery went well. You know what? You're actually more likable when you can't talk.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: But let's be honest, that's not saying much.

jokes.text: His dreadlocks remind me of the predator, Which is weird, because you're doing the actual hunting And you seem invisible to him.
jokes.setup: Commenting on guy Shirley likes
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference twisted to analyze real relationship dynamic
jokes.explanation: Peak Abed - uses pop culture reference to make insightful observation about social dynamics in perfectly character-specific way.

jokes.text: Before I talk, I ask myself, "what am I about to say And how might it affect each person listening?" I'm really glad you said that, britta. The idea that you compulsively filter yourself Makes your lack of flavor kind of a flavor.
jokes.setup: Britta explains her thoughtful approach to conversation
jokes.punchline: Abed immediately demonstrates the opposite by insulting her methodology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural joke - Abed violates Britta's rule immediately while making astute observation about her personality. Perfect character dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I always try to be thoughtful about how I communicate. I listen actively and consider the other person's perspective before responding.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's the most pretentious thing I've ever heard. You just like hearing yourself talk.

jokes.text: As far back as I can remember, I always wanted to be in a mafia movie.
jokes.setup: Jeff says their plan is like a mafia movie
jokes.punchline: Abed quotes Goodfellas opening line literally
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed movie reference, but the literalness makes it funny rather than just referential.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Our plan is like a mafia movie.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: As far back as I can remember, I always wanted to be a gangster.

jokes.text: That's exactly what it's like, Abed, And you get the most important job. You're gonna be fry cook. We may be watching different mafia movies.
jokes.setup: Jeff assigns Abed the 'most important job'
jokes.punchline: Abed points out fry cook isn't typically the don
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Jeff's grandiose framing - Abed's media literacy cuts through the BS.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Abed, I'm assigning you the most important job.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Fry cook isn't typically the don.

jokes.text: You want to be sheep, keep flocking. You want to be wolves, form a pack.
jokes.setup: Jeff giving inspirational speech
jokes.punchline: Generic motivational metaphor delivered with excessive confidence
jokes.explanation: Standard Jeff pomposity - not original but fits character well.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You know what life is? It's like a river. And we're all just boats floating down that river. Some boats are bigger, some boats are smaller, but we're all heading in the same direction. And that direction is... forward. You just have to believe in yourself, trust the current, and before you know it, you'll reach the ocean of your dreams. And that's the truth.

jokes.text: Trump tower, this is Donald. Well, he can deep-fry the hell out of chicken. I can tell you that.
jokes.setup: Troy pretending to be Donald Trump as reference for Abed
jokes.punchline: Trump enthusiastically endorsing chicken frying skills
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist bit - Trump endorsing chicken frying is appropriately ridiculous for the scheme.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You're doing an amazing job with that chicken. Believe me, I know fried chicken. Nobody fries chicken like you do. You're tremendous.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I give it a 10 out of 10. Actually, an 11. That's how good it is.

jokes.text: Would you like one? Name's Shirley. Memorize it.
jokes.setup: Shirley offering chicken finger to attractive guy
jokes.punchline: Suddenly aggressive power move
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Shirley's newfound confidence expressed through intimidation rather than charm.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Would you like a chicken finger?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: How about I shove it down your throat?

jokes.text: I dressed like a crazy pharaoh for you, man.
jokes.setup: Troy defending Abed from criticism
jokes.punchline: Absurd costume reference as evidence of loyalty
jokes.explanation: Random costume detail delivered as serious evidence of friendship - perfectly Troy.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You don't know Abed like I do. Last Halloween, he dressed up as a teapot. A teapot! Do you know how hard it is to be a teapot? There's no arm holes!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: But he did it. For the bit. That's loyalty. That's friendship.

jokes.text: Yeah, I agreed to give Cheng eight fingers per lunch In exchange for a 10% bump on every Spanish test For each of us.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining his agreement with Chang
jokes.punchline: Reveals organized academic corruption scheme
jokes.explanation: Shows how the operation has evolved into systematic corruption - good escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Chang and I have an understanding. He leaves me alone, I don't expose his entire organized academic corruption scheme.

jokes.text: Troy, why do you have a monkey? Uh, it's an animal that looks like a dude. Why don't I have ten of them?
jokes.setup: Question about why Troy has a monkey
jokes.punchline: Troy's logic leads to wanting ten monkeys
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic - simple observation leads to absurd escalation through childlike reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well, if one monkey is good, then ten monkeys would be ten times better!

jokes.text: I am a robot. I am programmed to love this backpack.
jokes.setup: Troy showing off his new backpack
jokes.punchline: Robot voice to express enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Cute Troy moment but not particularly original - robot voice is predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: This backpack has so many compartments, it's like... *robot voice* BEEP BOOP. MAXIMUM STORAGE CAPACITY REACHED.

jokes.text: More insane than programming them To replace auto workers? What?
jokes.setup: Someone calls the robot thing insane
jokes.punchline: Troy references actual labor displacement with robots
jokes.explanation: Unexpected moment of social awareness from Troy - subverts expectations nicely.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: He's not insane. He's just a victim of our society's obsession with automation and outsourcing. Do you know how many people have lost their jobs to robots?

jokes.text: Pierce's hackneyed entourage. Hackneyed? You see the turban guy? Yes, in a '90s ski movie That featured an entourage.
jokes.setup: Pierce questions the word 'hackneyed'
jokes.punchline: Jeff's very specific pop culture burn about clichéd diversity casting
jokes.explanation: Specific enough reference to feel earned, good Jeff condescension.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What does 'hackneyed' mean?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It means overused and clichéd, like when a TV show needs to seem diverse so they cast a sassy black woman, a wise-cracking Asian guy, and a token gay character.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: Isn't that right, Annie's boobs? Please rename that thing! And this time, not with a contest on Twitter!
jokes.setup: Troy addresses monkey by inappropriate name
jokes.punchline: Annie's frustration reveals this has happened before via Twitter contest
jokes.explanation: Good running gag escalation - Twitter contest detail adds layers to Annie's frustration.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Hey there, you little...

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Troy! We talked about this. You can't keep calling him that!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What? It's just a nickname!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: A nickname? Troy, that's the third time this month. Do you know how many complaints I got on Twitter from the contest winners? This is exactly why we had those rules about the monkey in the first place!

jokes.text: You wouldn't threaten a monkey. They have more lactic acid than us.
jokes.setup: Conflict over the monkey
jokes.punchline: Troy's defense based on made-up monkey physiology
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy pseudo-science delivered with complete confidence - absurd but character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, okay. You don't understand monkey physiology. When a monkey feels threatened, it goes into what's called a 'defensive cluster.' The fur puffs up, the tail curls, and it emits a high-pitched screech that can temporarily stun a human nervous system.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That monkey was protecting itself! And frankly, I think we all owe it an apology.

jokes.text: Streets ahead!
jokes.setup: Pierce still trying to make the phrase happen
jokes.punchline: Persistent use despite earlier failure
jokes.explanation: Solid callback - Pierce's persistence makes the running gag work.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I know you guys didn't like it the first time, but hear me out. Streets ahead is going to catch on. It's catchy, it's cool, it's got that ring to it. Streets ahead!

jokes.text: Your ego. Well... I see. I see. This has been about me the whole time.
jokes.setup: Abed tells Jeff his ego is the problem
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately makes it about himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of the exact flaw being discussed - Jeff proves Abed's point immediately.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Your ego is the problem.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: My ego? That's not fair. I have plenty of other problems too.

jokes.text: Oh, and for your information, I don't have an ego. My facebook photo is a landscape.
jokes.setup: Jeff denying he has an ego
jokes.punchline: Humble-brag evidence that actually shows vanity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jeff line - perfectly captures his vanity disguised as humility. Highly quotable and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I don't have an ego. I'm actually incredibly humble about my achievements.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Like, just yesterday someone told me I was the most attractive person they'd ever met, and I was like, 'No, that's not true.' But they insisted.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And when I got my law degree, I specifically asked them not to mention it was with honors. I just wanted it to be about the learning.

jokes.text: You want a shot at the Jeff winger throne? You better bring a powerful ass.
jokes.setup: Jeff and Abed are having a power struggle
jokes.punchline: Jeff's weirdly specific threat about needing a 'powerful ass' to challenge him
jokes.explanation: Jeff's attempt to sound tough comes out as bizarrely specific and somewhat sexual

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You think you can challenge me? You'd need a powerful ass to do that.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I have a powerful ass.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, you don't.

jokes.text: Can we make this quick? I need to give a banana to Annie's boobs.
jokes.setup: Troy needs to leave the meeting
jokes.punchline: His innocent reason sounds sexual due to the monkey's unfortunate name
jokes.explanation: Classic innocent-but-sounds-dirty humor, enhanced by the running gag

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I have to go. I need to feed my monkey.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: His name is Aids.

jokes.text: Say "streets ahead" and die.
jokes.setup: Pierce about to say his phrase again
jokes.punchline: Jeff's exasperated death threat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag - economical threat.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Catch you on the flip side!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: If you say that one more time, I will kill you.

jokes.text: Streets ahead.
jokes.setup: Jeff just threatened him for saying it
jokes.punchline: Pierce immediately says it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce defiance - shows he won't be intimidated even by death threats.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: It anyway.

jokes.text: Yeah, I got you fired. You deserved it. Maybe. Maybe not. The question is: What do they deserve?
jokes.setup: Jeff admits to getting Star-Burns fired
jokes.punchline: Star-Burns ominously shifts focus to revenge
jokes.explanation: Good villain turn setup - ominous without being too dramatic.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I may have gotten you fired.

dialogue.speaker: Star-Burns
dialogue.line: Oh, so you admit it?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Star-Burns
dialogue.line: Well then, I guess we're done here. I'm going to go ruin your life now.

jokes.text: Yeah, I got you fired. You deserved it. Maybe. Maybe not. The question is: What do they deserve?
jokes.setup: Star-Burns confronting Jeff about getting him fired
jokes.punchline: Shifts from personal grievance to threatening the group
jokes.explanation: Good villain moment showing Star-Burns' revenge plot

dialogue.speaker: Star-Burns
dialogue.line: You got me fired, Jeff! You think you can just destroy someone's life?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Star-Burns, come on, you were selling drugs out of the library—

dialogue.speaker: Star-Burns
dialogue.line: That's not the point! The point is I'm done being nice about this. You want to know what I know about you people? I know things. Things that could hurt all of you.

jokes.text: No, no, no, no. My monkey hates this caviar.
jokes.setup: Troy complaining to Abed about something
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's feeding caviar to his monkey
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of how spoiled they've become - feeding expensive food to animals.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Abed, I need to talk to you about something serious.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: What's wrong?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I've been having some... financial difficulties lately.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: How bad are we talking?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well, I've had to make some tough choices. I'm feeding caviar to my monkey.

jokes.text: Annie's boobs could be anywhere. Annie's boobs could be on the side of the road. We got it. The monkey's name is 'annie's boobs.'
jokes.setup: Troy worries about the missing monkey using its unfortunate name
jokes.punchline: Jeff's exasperated explanation to the audience about why they keep saying this
jokes.explanation: Meta-textual moment where Jeff breaks the fourth wall to address the running gag

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We have to find Señor Scratchy! Señor Scratchy is missing!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yes, the monkey's name is unfortunately Señor Scratchy. We're aware of how it sounds. We're going to keep saying it anyway because that's his name and we're professionals.

jokes.text: That little arab is off his rocker. He released Annie's boobs. Annie's boobs could be anywhere. Annie's boobs could be on the side of the road.
jokes.setup: Pierce complaining about Abed releasing the monkey
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic scenarios about monkey's fate using inappropriate name
jokes.explanation: Running gag gets a bit repetitive but Pierce's drama adds some value.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: That monkey could be anywhere by now! It could be in the vents, it could be in the dean's office, it could be in my pants!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: And when they find it, they're gonna say 'We found Pierce's monkey in Pierce's pants,' and nobody's gonna believe me that Abed put it there!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: By tomorrow, the headline's gonna be 'Local Man's Monkey Problem Gets Out of Hand.'

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Next week: 'Pierce's Monkey Bites Child.' Week after: 'Pierce's Monkey Elected Mayor.'

jokes.text: You were right. Now what you can do is go home And write that on your bathroom mirror. Wouldn't that make it seem like I was right? Yeah, because it would be my mirror. It's not gonna be reversed because you're the one who--
jokes.setup: Jeff wants Britta to acknowledge he was right
jokes.punchline: Confusion over mirror physics leads to abandoned explanation
jokes.explanation: Decent bit about mirror confusion but explanation gets muddled.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So you're admitting I was right?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I'm not admitting anything.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You literally just said—

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: What I said was that if you looked in a mirror and saw yourself looking back, that would mean the mirror was working.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Right. Exactly. So I was right.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: No, that's not what that means. If the mirror's working, it means light is traveling to you from the mirror, which means—okay, never mind.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What? No, keep going.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I can't explain it.

jokes.text: It's not that complicated, Jeff. They replaced the "c" in circle with a "z" for zucchini.
jokes.setup: Jeff doesn't understand 'zucchini zircles'
jokes.punchline: Abed explains obvious wordplay with undeserved confidence
jokes.explanation: Good Abed moment - explaining something obvious like it's complex shows his literal mindset.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's zucchini... in circles. Zucchini circles. Zircles.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I get it.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's a play on words.

jokes.text: I just need to be able to connect to people like you can, And then I can make everyone happy Like you do.
jokes.setup: Abed explaining his motivation
jokes.punchline: Thinks Jeff makes everyone happy when he clearly doesn't
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Abed's misreading of Jeff's social skills is endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I think Jeff makes everyone happy when he clearly doesn't.

jokes.text: By the way, they started using that As a seasonal arc on law & order. Total rip off.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jeff's fake degree
jokes.punchline: Abed claims Law & Order stole their storyline
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary - treating their lives as content that gets adapted.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Law & Order stole our storyline.

jokes.text: Like knight rider. Exactly. Like knight rider. I'll put on a leather jacket And make out with an aerobics instructor. You pull around front and comedically Startle passersby with your ability to talk.
jokes.setup: Jeff agrees their partnership is like Knight Rider
jokes.punchline: Assigns ridiculous literal Knight Rider roles to each other
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the metaphor into literal absurdity - both characters commit to the bit.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, so you're Michael Knight and I'm KITT.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: No way. You're Michael Knight.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Why would I be Michael Knight? I'm clearly the intelligent one.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Michael Knight is the human. He's the lead.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: KITT is the lead. KITT does all the work. Michael Knight just drives around looking concerned.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Fine. You're KITT.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'll be Devon Miles.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Why do you get to be Devon Miles?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Because I'm the only one who can pull off the ascot.

jokes.text: Can we eat them while sitting on a table Like in sixteen candles? Pick one reference, Abed.
jokes.setup: Abed suggests another movie reference scenario
jokes.punchline: Jeff calls out his reference overuse
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - Jeff learning to manage Abed's reference tendency.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: What if this was like that scene from Die Hard where—

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Abed, every single thing you say is a movie reference. Can you just talk like a normal person for once?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not even a movie reference, that's just—

jokes.text: Do you know who might have stolen A box of hairnets from the kitchen? Someone with hair. I'm gonna write that down.
jokes.setup: Security asking about stolen hairnets
jokes.punchline: Abed's obvious answer treated as valuable intel
jokes.explanation: Good ending bit - Abed's literal thinking treated as investigative insight.

dialogue.speaker: Security
dialogue.line: Do you know anything about the missing hairnets from the cafeteria?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Well, they're hairnets. People wear them on their heads.

dialogue.speaker: Security
dialogue.line: Yes, but where are they?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Presumably on people's heads.

tmdb_overview: A scheme to replace a corrupt cafeteria employee transforms the study group into a criminal gang run by a power-hungry Abed.
insight: Poultry heist escalates absurdly while maintaining character logic throughout.
